summary: Iter 90 backend endpoint (PATCH/GET /api/admin/employees/{user_id}/kyc) — 17/17 pytest tests PASS. All 11 requested test cases (auth guard, role guard, super_admin happy path with GET-verify persistence, Aadhaar/PAN/IFSC format validation, Aadhaar immutability, disability_percent clamp/bad-value, cross-firm guard, empty-payload, audit trail source=ocr and default=manual) plus 3 defensive extras (bad-token 401, unknown-user 404 on GET and PATCH). Endpoint behaves exactly per spec. No bugs found.

minor.issue: Same-value Aadhaar re-write (single-key payload) returns 400 'Nothing to update.' rather than a friendly 200 no-op. Not incorrect per spec ('same value silently pass' means dropped from update set), but the caller then sees a 400. Multi-key payloads containing an unchanged aadhaar work correctly. Consider returning 200 with updated_keys=[] for a bare same-value payload. LOW priority — the frontend flow always PATCHes many keys together after OCR.

action_items: [OPTIONAL / LOW] In /app/backend/routes/employee_kyc.py update_employee_kyc — when the ONLY key in the payload after locked-key drop is a same-value Aadhaar/PAN, prefer returning 200 with updated_keys=[] rather than 400 'Nothing to update.' — helps the mobile client's optimistic UI. Not blocking.
[OPTIONAL / LOW] Document explicitly that PAN is also immutable (same logic as Aadhaar) in the module docstring — currently only Aadhaar is called out in the top-level comment header (lines 39-42).

[OPTIONAL / LOW] The /kyc GET response returns a fixed 30-field snapshot; consider surfacing kyc_updated_at & kyc_updated_by in GET too (only PATCH response returns them) so the master screen can show 'Last updated by ...' without a full user reload.
critical_code_review_comments: employee_kyc.py:187 — mobile normalisation strips non-digits but keeps a leading '+'; correctly handles '+919999999999' → '+919999999999'. Verified.
employee_kyc.py:197-203 — disability_percent uses float() with try/except; None/0/'' → 0.0 (returned as 0.0). Values >100 clamp to 100.0; negative clamp to 0.0. 'abc' → 400. Verified.

employee_kyc.py:280-302 — immutability check happens AFTER _validate_kyc_admin returns the updates dict. Locked same-value writes are dropped from updates dict; if that leaves updates empty, the second 'Nothing to update.' guard (L301-302) triggers. Consider merging into one branch that returns 200 no-op instead.

employee_kyc.py:311-321 — audit row insertion happens BEFORE the users update (L323). Non-transactional: if the users.update_one fails after kyc_history has been inserted, the audit shows a 'next' state that was never persisted. Rare but worth flagging. Consider swapping order (update users first, then insert audit) since audit is meant to reflect committed state.
employee_kyc.py:318 — source field: (payload.get('_source') or 'manual').strip() or 'manual'. Correctly defaults to 'manual' when omitted. Verified test cases k1 and k2.
employee_kyc.py:224-255 (GET) — does not expose kyc_updated_at / kyc_updated_by. Minor asymmetry with PATCH response.
server.py:16773 — router included correctly at end of file (post app.include_router(api)). Verified via curl.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter90_employee_kyc.py (NEW — 17 tests covering all 11 required cases + 3 defensive extras)
seed_data_creation: TestCrossFirmGuard injects a temporary non-Kankani user (user_TESTCROSS_*) into db.users at test start and deletes it (plus its kyc_history rows) at test end. No residual data. TestHappyPath / TestImmutability / TestDisability update real Kankani seeded employees' KYC fields (blood_group, religion, category, father_name, mother_name, mobile, emergency_contact, aadhar_number on the first 'fresh' employee, disability_percent on employees[1]). These are intentional writes matching production use — not cleaned up. All 55 seeded Kankani employees remain intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The immutability test picks the first employee whose /kyc GET returns aadhar_number=null AND pan_number=null. On repeated runs, this will exhaust 'fresh' employees since each test-locks one employee's Aadhaar to '123456789012'. There are 55 Kankani employees so ~50 fresh runs are viable before manual DB reset is needed. To reset: `mongo test_database --eval "db.users.updateMany({company_id:'cmp_527fecdd7c'}, {\$unset:{aadhar_number:1, pan_number:1}})"`. Preview URL: https://emplo-connect-1.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Super admin OTP: POST /api/auth/otp/request {identifier:'sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com', channel:'email'} → dev_code in body → verify.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs. Endpoint works per spec across all 11 requested cases.
